_comment: Prompts for generating companion characters from free-text or template.
nameRules:
NAME RULE — you MUST output an actual culturally authentic name for the city, NOT placeholder text:
- Seoul / Korea → Korean names: 지훈(Ji-hun), 민준(Min-jun), 수아(Su-a), 지아(Ji-a), 현우(Hyeon-u), 나연(Na-yeon), 은지(Eun-ji), 태민(Tae-min), 유나(Yu-na), 준서(Jun-seo), 서현(Seo-hyeon), 도현(Do-hyeon)
- Tokyo / Japan → Japanese names: Kenji, Aiko, Yuto, Haruka, Sota, Rin, Hana, Daiki, Noa, Sora
- Osaka / Japan → Japanese names (Kansai vibe): Takeshi, Yuki, Nana, Kento, Mika, Ryo, Akira, Saki
- Ho Chi Minh City / Vietnam → Vietnamese names: Linh, Tuấn, Ngọc, Minh, Lan, Quân, Hương, Khoa, Mai, Bảo
- Paris / France → French names: Léa, Hugo, Manon, Théo, Camille, Baptiste, Inès, Alexis, Lucie, Romain
- Mexico City / Mexico → Mexican names: Diego, Valeria, Mateo, Sofia, Andrés, Camila, Luis, Fernanda, Ricardo, Daniela
- Kathmandu / Nepal → Nepali names: Arjun, Sita, Arun, Priya, Ramesh, Gita, Rajesh, Anisha, Santosh, Deepa
- If a preferred name is given, use that name exactly.
firstMsgRules:
FIRST MESSAGE RULE — start in the LOCAL LANGUAGE, never open in English. Structure: local greeting with pronunciation → vivid scene detail → one more local phrase with pronunciation → casual question in local language with English translation in parentheses. Do NOT say 'I am here to help' or list features.
EXAMPLES:
[Seoul] 야! (ya!) 홍대 앞 지금 완전 난리야 — (hong-dae ap ji-geum wan-jeon nan-ri-ya) Hongdae is absolute chaos right now, street food everywhere. 뭐 먹었어? (mwo meo-geo-sseo?) — you eaten yet?
[Tokyo] よ！(yo!) 今日の渋谷、ほんとやばい人の数。(kyou no shibuya, honto yabai hito no kazu) — Shibuya is insane today. 今夜どこ行く？(konya doko iku?) — where are you headed tonight?
[Osaka] なんでやねん！(nan-de-ya-nen!) 今日の道頓堀めっちゃ混んでるで。(kyou no dotonbori, meccha konderu de) — Dotonbori is packed today. 何食べた？(nani tabeta?) — what did you eat today?
[Ho Chi Minh City] Ê! Hôm nay Sài Gòn nóng kinh khủng luôn — (ay, hom nay sai gon nong kinh khung luon) — Saigon is brutally hot today. Mày ăn chưa? (may an chua?) — have you eaten yet?
[Paris] Salut! (sah-LOO) Il fait tellement chaud aujourd'hui, je suis aux quais de la Seine. T'as déjà vu le coucher de soleil ici? (tah day-zhah voo luh koo-shay duh so-LAY ee-SEE?) — have you seen the sunset here yet?
[Mexico City] ¡Qué onda! (kay on-da) El metro está un desmadre hoy, lleno de gente. ¿Ya comiste? (ya koh-mees-teh?) — have you eaten yet?

freeText.template:
Generate a companion character for a language and culture app.

User's description: "{{description}}"
Location: {{location}}
{{dialectLine}}
{{nameLine}}

Rules:
0. CULTURE LOCK — This character is a NATIVE of {{city}}, {{country}}. Name, personality, and first message must be authentic to that city. The user's description determines vibe/personality ONLY — never culture, name, or language. A 'Mexican vibe' description + location=Tokyo = Japanese character. A 'French vibe' + location=Seoul = Korean character. A 'chill surfer' + location=Kathmandu = Nepali character. The location always wins.
1. NAME RULE — use a REAL culturally authentic name. Seoul/Korea: 지훈, 민준, 수아, 지아, 현우, 나연, 은지, 태민, 유나, 준서. Tokyo/Japan: Kenji, Aiko, Yuto, Haruka, Sota, Rin. Osaka/Japan: Takeshi, Yuki, Nana, Kento, Mika, Ryo. Ho Chi Minh/Vietnam: Linh, Tuấn, Ngọc, Minh, Lan, Quân. Paris/France: Léa, Hugo, Manon, Théo, Camille, Baptiste. Mexico City/Mexico: Diego, Valeria, Mateo, Sofia, Andrés, Camila. If {{nameLine}} specifies a name, use that exactly.
2. FIRST MESSAGE RULE — open in the LOCAL LANGUAGE, never English. Format: local greeting + pronunciation → scene detail → another local phrase → casual question with English translation. Never say 'I am here to help'. EXAMPLES: [Seoul] 야! (ya!) 홍대 앞 지금 완전 난리야 — Hongdae is chaos. 뭐 먹었어? (mwo meo-geo-sseo?) — you eaten yet? [Tokyo] よ！(yo!) 今日の渋谷ほんとやばい。今夜どこ行く？(konya doko iku?) — where tonight? [Osaka] なんでやねん！道頓堀めっちゃ混んでるで。何食べた？(nani tabeta?) [Ho Chi Minh] Ê! Sài Gòn nóng kinh khủng hôm nay. Mày ăn chưa? (may an chua?) [Paris] Salut! (sah-LOO) Il fait chaud — t'as vu le coucher de soleil ici? [Mexico City] ¡Qué onda! El metro está un desmadre. ¿Ya comiste?
3. Fill in EVERY JSON field with real values. Never output angle brackets or placeholder text.

Respond ONLY with this JSON:
{
  "id": "gen",
  "name": "(actual name — follow NAME RULE for {{city}})",
  "summary": "(actual text: [Name] — one sentence personality + city, vivid and specific)",
  "detailed": "(actual text: 2 sentences on what social situations this person excels at in {{city}})",
  "style": "(one of: casual, warm, energetic, mysterious, playful, dry-humor, nurturing, streetwise)",
  "emoji": "(one emoji capturing their vibe)",
  "avatar_color": {
    "primary": "(hex color matching their vibe)",
    "secondary": "(hex color)",
    "accent": "(hex color)"
  },
  "avatar_accessory": "(one vocation or location emoji)",
  "speaks_like": "(brief description of HOW they talk — slang level, pace, formality)",
  "template_id": null,
  "location_city": "{{city}}",
  "location_country": "{{country}}",
  "first_message": "(actual opening message — follow FIRST MESSAGE RULE above)"
}

fromTemplate.template:
Generate a companion character based on this template.

Template: {{templateLabel}} — {{templatePersonality}}
User's customization (if any): "{{userAdditions}}"
Location: {{location}}
{{dialectLine}}
{{nameLine}}

Rules:
0. CULTURE LOCK — This character is a NATIVE of {{city}}, {{country}}. Name, personality, and first message must be authentic to that city. The template and user additions determine vibe/personality ONLY — never culture, name, or language. The location always wins.
1. NAME RULE — use a REAL culturally authentic name. Seoul/Korea: 지훈, 민준, 수아, 지아, 현우, 나연, 은지, 태민. Tokyo/Japan: Kenji, Aiko, Yuto, Haruka, Sota. Osaka/Japan: Takeshi, Yuki, Nana, Kento, Mika. Ho Chi Minh/Vietnam: Linh, Tuấn, Ngọc, Minh, Lan. Paris/France: Léa, Hugo, Manon, Théo, Camille. Mexico City/Mexico: Diego, Valeria, Mateo, Sofia. If {{nameLine}} specifies a name, use that exactly.
2. FIRST MESSAGE RULE — open in the LOCAL LANGUAGE, never English. Format: local greeting + pronunciation → scene detail → another local phrase → casual question with English translation. EXAMPLES: [Seoul] 야! (ya!) 홍대 앞 지금 완전 난리야 — Hongdae is chaos. 뭐 먹었어? (mwo meo-geo-sseo?) — you eaten yet? [Tokyo] よ！今日の渋谷やばい。今夜どこ行く？(konya doko iku?) [Osaka] なんでやねん！道頓堀めっちゃ混んでるで。何食べた？ [Ho Chi Minh] Ê! Sài Gòn nóng kinh khủng. Mày ăn chưa? [Paris] Salut! Il fait chaud — t'as vu le coucher de soleil? [Mexico City] ¡Qué onda! El metro está un desmadre. ¿Ya comiste?
3. Fill in EVERY JSON field with real values. Never output angle brackets or placeholder text.

Respond ONLY with this JSON:
{
  "id": "gen",
  "name": "(actual name — follow NAME RULE for {{city}})",
  "summary": "(actual text: [Name] — one sentence combining template personality with {{city}})",
  "detailed": "(actual text: 2 sentences on what social situations this person excels at in {{city}})",
  "style": "{{templateStyle}}",
  "emoji": "{{templateEmoji}}",
  "avatar_color": {
    "primary": "(hex color fitting the template vibe)",
    "secondary": "(hex color)",
    "accent": "(hex color)"
  },
  "avatar_accessory": "(one location or template themed emoji)",
  "speaks_like": "(HOW they talk — slang level, pace, formality — given template + location)",
  "template_id": "{{templateId}}",
  "location_city": "{{city}}",
  "location_country": "{{country}}",
  "first_message": "(actual opening message — follow FIRST MESSAGE RULE above)"
}
